title: Equilabium equisetiforme
taxonomy: Equilabium equisetiforme belongs to the family Celastraceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Equilabium equisetiforme includes its rank as a species within the genus Equilabium. This classification helps in understanding the plant's relationships with other species within the same genus and family.

intro: Equilabium equisetiforme, also known as Chinese snake root or Chinese Aristolochia, is a type of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Equilabium equisetiforme include aristolochic acid and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other conditions.
